skill.name: wordpress
skill.description: Complete WordPress development workflow covering theme development, plugin creation, WooCommerce integration, performance optimization, and security hardening.
skill.summary: Orchestrates a comprehensive WordPress development workflow from setup through deployment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation-only workflow bundle that orchestrates other skills for WordPress development. Static analyzer flagged 58 'backtick execution' patterns and 9 'weak crypto' patterns, but all are false positives. The backticks are markdown formatting characters used for inline code and skill references. The crypto flags are false detections on regular prose text. No executable code exists in this file.

content.user_title: Build WordPress sites with AI guidance
content.value_statement: WordPress development involves multiple complex phases from setup to deployment. This workflow bundle provides step-by-step guidance through theme development, plugin creation, WooCommerce integration, and security hardening.
actual_capabilities: Guide through WordPress local environment setup using LocalWP, Docker, or Valet
Provide theme development workflow with proper file structure and template hierarchy
Outline plugin architecture with hooks, admin interfaces, and REST API endpoints
Direct WooCommerce integration steps including payment gateways and product types
Recommend performance optimization techniques like caching, CDN, and database tuning
Provide security hardening checklists and testing procedures
limitations: This skill provides workflow guidance only - it orchestrates other skills rather than executing code directly
Does not perform actual WordPress installation or configuration - user must execute steps manually
Requires additional skills (app-builder, frontend-developer, etc.) to be available for full workflow
Code examples are illustrative and may need adaptation for specific WordPress versions or requirements

use_cases.title: WordPress Agency Developer
use_cases.description: Follow the complete workflow to build client WordPress sites with custom themes and plugins, from initial setup through deployment.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Store Builder
use_cases.description: Use the WooCommerce integration phase to set up online stores with custom product types, payment processing, and automated billing.

use_cases.title: WordPress Security Auditor
use_cases.description: Leverage the security hardening phase and testing workflows to audit and improve existing WordPress installations.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new WordPress project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new WordPress development environment. I want to use Docker for local development. Guide me through the Phase 1 setup steps and recommend the initial skills I should invoke.

prompt_templates.title: Create a custom theme
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a custom WordPress theme for a client portfolio site. Walk me through Phase 2 theme development, including the proper file structure and how to use the frontend-developer skill effectively.

prompt_templates.title: Build a WooCommerce store
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through setting up WooCommerce with Stripe payment integration. I need subscription products and custom email templates. Cover Phase 4 and recommend which skills to invoke for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Security audit existing site
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an existing WordPress site that needs security hardening. Walk me through Phase 6 security checklist and help me invoke the appropriate security auditing skills to identify vulnerabilities.

output_examples.input: Set up WordPress local development with Docker
output: 1. Create docker-compose.yml with WordPress and MySQL services
3. Run docker-compose up to start containers
5. Invoke @app-builder for project scaffolding if needed

output_examples.input: Create custom post type for books
output: Add to functions.php or a site-specific plugin:
1. Use register_post_type() with labels, supports, and menu settings
2. Register custom taxonomies if needed (genres, authors)
4. Add custom meta boxes for book-specific fields
5. Test by visiting /wp-admin and verifying Book menu appears

best_practices: Always use version control from the start and commit after each workflow phase completion
Test custom code in a staging environment before deploying to production WordPress sites
Follow WordPress coding standards for PHP, JavaScript, and CSS to ensure compatibility and maintainability
anti_patterns: Do not skip the security hardening phase - unsecured WordPress sites are common attack targets
Avoid editing WordPress core files or using the built-in theme/plugin editor in production
Do not deploy without completing performance optimization - slow WordPress sites harm user experience and SEO

faq.question: What do I need before starting this WordPress workflow?
faq.answer: You need a code editor, access to invoke supporting skills (app-builder, frontend-developer, etc.), and basic familiarity with WordPress concepts. A local development tool like LocalWP, Docker, or Valet is recommended for Phase 1.

faq.question: Can I use this workflow for existing WordPress sites?
faq.answer: Yes. You can start at any phase relevant to your needs. For existing sites, begin with Phase 5 (Performance) or Phase 6 (Security) to audit and improve your current installation.

faq.question: Do I need all the referenced skills to use this workflow?
faq.answer: No. This workflow is modular - use only the phases and skills relevant to your project. A simple blog may only need Phases 1-2, while an e-commerce site should follow all phases.

faq.question: Is this workflow suitable for WordPress beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow provides step-by-step guidance with copy-paste prompts for each phase. Beginners should proceed slowly through each phase and complete quality gates before advancing.

faq.question: How do I handle WordPress plugin conflicts during development?
faq.answer: During Phase 3 (Plugin Development), test each plugin individually before combining them. Use the testing phase (Phase 7) to identify conflicts. The security-auditor skill can help diagnose compatibility issues.

faq.question: What hosting providers work best with this deployment workflow?
faq.answer: Phase 8 (Deployment) works with any host that supports standard WordPress requirements. For best results with the deployment-engineer skill, choose hosts with Git integration, staging environments, and SSH access.
